description: v3-deep retrain attempt with K=10 gen=8 popSize=24 matchesPerEval=400 sigma=0.6 per tier (3 tiers, total 90 candidates, 27min pop-gen + 19min tournament)
finding: v3-deep retrain did NOT improve over v2 weights + v3 memory architecture. Tournament-wide tier-vs-tier average: hard vs normal = 48.8%, hard vs easy = 48.4%, normal vs easy = 49.7% — meaning trained "hard tier" candidates do NOT systematically beat "easy tier" candidates. ES finds robust cautious strategies under memory constraint, defeating the tier-grading intent.

deployed_combo_v2_weights_v3_memory.note: v2-deep trained weights (commit 2b15105) running under v3 memory mask architecture

conclusion: v2 weights + v3 memory remains best. v3-deep training data (90 candidates) retained in population.json/ranking.json for future reference.
